sections.title: Lesson Summary (பாடச் சுருக்கம்)
sections.content: The story 'Eidgah' by Munshi Premchand portrays the pure and selfless love of a four-year-old orphan boy, Hamid, towards his grandmother, Ameena. On the festive morning of Eid, while his friends Mahmood, Mohsin, Noorey, and Sammi spend their money on merry-go-round rides, clay toys, and sweets, Hamid controls his desires. Having only three paisas, he thinks about his grandmother who burns her fingers every day while making chappatis. Hamid bravely negotiates with a shopkeeper and buys a pair of iron tongs for her. Initially scolded by Ameena for not buying anything to eat or play with, she soon realizes his immense love and selflessness, bursting into tears of joy. The tongs become as precious as silver to her.

sections.title: Important Concepts: Section I - The Morning of Eid (பாடக்கருத்துக்கள்)
sections.content: The morning of Eid was beautiful, with a pink glow in the sky. The village was filled with excitement as everyone prepared to go to the Eidgah. Among the boys, Hamid was the happiest despite being poorly dressed and an orphan (his father died of cholera, and his mother also passed away). He lived with his Granny Ameena and believed his parents had gone to fetch gifts from Allah. His friends Mahmood had 12 coins and Mohsin had 15 coins. Granny Ameena was sad as she had no grains in the house on Eid. Hamid ran ahead of everyone eagerly towards the fair.

sections.title: Important Concepts: Section II - At the Fair (பாடக்கருத்துக்கள்)
sections.content: After the Eid prayers, the men embraced each other and the crowd rushed to the sweets and toy vendors. Hamid's friends enjoyed 25 rounds of the merry-go-round for one paisa. Then they bought clay toys costing two paisas each: Mahmood bought a policeman, Mohsin bought a water-carrier, and Noorey bought a lawyer. Hamid, having only three paisas, watched them hungrily as he couldn't afford expensive toys that would break easily. Next, his friends relished sweets like sesame seed candy, gulab-jamuns, and halva, leaving Hamid out.

sections.title: Important Concepts: Section III - Hamid's Gift (பாடக்கருத்துக்கள்)
sections.content: Passing the sweet shops, Hamid stopped at a hardware store. He remembered his granny burning her fingers while cooking chappatis. He asked the price of a pair of tongs. The shopkeeper initially said six paisas, then five, but Hamid asked if he could give it for three. To his surprise, the shopkeeper agreed. Hamid proudly carried it like a gun. He proved to his friends that his iron tongs were like a 'tiger among toys' because they wouldn't break. Upon returning home, Granny Ameena scolded him for starving and buying an iron tool, but when Hamid explained he bought it to save her fingers, she was deeply moved and cried.

sections.title: Glossary (கலைச்சொற்கள்)
sections.content:
• Dazzling – extremely bright
• Tattered – torn
• Suburbs – outlying area
• Gaily – in bright colours
• Gaped – stared
• Smack your lips – open and close lips noisily to show enjoyment
• Relished – enjoyed or took pleasure in the taste
• Tongs – a tool used to hold hot things
• Precious – valuable
• Wretched – unhappy

sections.title: Book Back 1-Mark Questions (பாட பின்புற வினாக்கள்)
sections.content:
• 'Finally the day had come'. Here the day refers to - Eid
• With their treasure, the boys would buy - toys and rubber balls in Eidgah.
• Hamid's friends are - Mahmood, Mohsin, Noorey and Sammi.
• 'If only her son were there...' What had happened to her son? - Her son had died.
• Hamid was like one with wings on his feet. This means - he ran fast.
• Hamid thought that his grandma would be pleased if he bought - a pair of tongs.
• Hamid's heart sank because the price of the tongs was - too high for him.
• 'My tongs are like a tiger among toys.' It means - Hamid valued his toy.
• Hamid's granny scolded him for buying iron tongs. Then she understood that Hamid had bought it - out of love and concern.

sections.title: Vocabulary and Grammar (தகவல் பேழை மற்றும் இலக்கணம்)
sections.content:
▸ Prefix and Suffix:
• - Suffixes (-ship, -hood, -less, -ful, -ment, -ous, -ion): childhood, wonderful, countless, relationship, excitement, dangerous, perfection.
• - Prefixes (un-, after-, im-): afternoon, impatient, unhappy.

▸ Quantity Words:
• - a cupful of sugar
• - a handful of grains
• - a spoonful of coffee
• - a mouthful of cake
• - a pocketful of chocolates
• - a bagful of silver/gold.

▸ Determiners (Grammar):
• - Many / Much: 'Many' is used for countable nouns (e.g., many apples). 'Much' is used for uncountable nouns (e.g., much traffic).
• - Some / Any: 'Some' is used in positive sentences (e.g., some biscuits). 'Any' is used in negative sentences or questions (e.g., any mangoes, isn't any water).

sections.title: Do You Know? (உங்களுக்கு தெரியுமா?)
sections.content: There are 109 members in Marapanhalli Laxmayha family who live in Karnataka's Kolar district. It is one among the last few purely joint families in India. 85 members of this massive family live under a single roof that spans 20 rooms. The other 24 live in the fields next to the house.

sections.title: Remember (நினைவில் கொள்க)
sections.content:
• Hamid is a 4-year-old orphan living with Ameena.
• Toys bought: Mahmood - Policeman, Mohsin - Water-carrier, Noorey - Lawyer.
• Hamid's wealth: Only 3 paisas.
• Price of Tongs: Reduced from 6 paisas to 3 paisas.

questions.question: Who is the author of the story 'Eidgah'?
questions.explanation: The story 'Eidgah' was originally written by Munshi Premchand and translated/adapted by Uma Raman.

questions.question: Which festival brings excitement to the village in the story?
questions.explanation: The story takes place on the morning of Eid, where everyone is excited to go to the Eidgah.

questions.question: How old was Hamid in the story?
questions.explanation: Hamid was only four years old, thin, and poorly dressed.

questions.question: With whom did Hamid live after his parents passed away?
questions.explanation: After his father died of cholera and his mother passed away, Hamid lived with his old Granny Ameena.

questions.question: How many coins did Mahmood have as his treasure?
questions.explanation: Mahmood counted his treasure and had a total of twelve coins.

questions.question: How many coins did Mohsin have?
questions.explanation: Mohsin counted his money and found he had fifteen coins.

questions.question: What reason was Hamid given regarding his mother's absence?
options: She had gone to the Eidgah alone.
She went to the neighboring village.
She had gone to Allah to get lovely gifts for him.
questions.explanation: Granny Ameena told Hamid that his mother had gone to Allah to get lovely gifts, which made him very happy.

questions.question: Why was Granny Ameena sad on the morning of Eid?
options: She did not have even a handful of grains.
She could not buy new clothes for herself.
questions.explanation: Ameena was sad because it was Eid and there was not even a handful of grains in the house to celebrate.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'tattered'?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'tattered' as torn. Hamid's cap was soiled and tattered.

questions.question: Which of the following fruits were abundant in the gardens along the road to the town?
questions.explanation: On both sides of the road in the suburbs, the mango and leechee trees were full of fruits.

questions.question: From the book back questions: 'Finally the day had come'. Here the day refers to ________.
questions.explanation: The children had been waiting for the festival of Eid, and finally, the day had arrived.

questions.question: From the book back questions: With their treasure, the boys would buy ________ in Eidgah.
questions.explanation: With their collected coins, the boys planned to buy countless things like toys, sweets, paper-pipes, and rubber balls.

questions.question: Who among the following was NOT one of Hamid's friends mentioned in the story?
questions.explanation: Hamid's group of friends consisted of Mahmood, Mohsin, Noorey, and Sammi. Raghul is not in the story.

questions.question: 'Hamid was like one with wings on his feet.' What does this mean?
options: He was flying like a bird.
questions.explanation: The phrase means he ran very fast out of extreme eagerness and excitement.

questions.question: What is the synonym for 'dazzling'?
questions.explanation: According to the glossary, 'dazzling' means extremely bright.

questions.question: How many rounds of fun did the boys get on the merry-go-round for one paisa?
questions.explanation: The merry-go-round cost one paisa and offered twenty-five rounds of fun.

questions.question: Why did Hamid stay away from the merry-go-round?
options: He had only three paisas and didn't want to waste them.
questions.explanation: Hamid had only three paisas in total, and he couldn't part with a third of his treasure for a few miserable rounds.

questions.question: Which toy did Mahmood buy at the fair?
questions.explanation: Mahmood bought a policeman in khaki with a red turban and a gun on his shoulder.

questions.question: Which toy did Noorey choose?
questions.explanation: Noorey got a lawyer toy, while Mohsin bought a water-carrier.

questions.question: What was the cost of each clay toy bought by Hamid's friends?
questions.explanation: Those clay toys cost two paisas each.

questions.question: Why didn't Hamid buy any of the clay toys?
options: They were expensive and would smash to bits if dropped.
His grandmother told him not to buy toys.
questions.explanation: Hamid thought they were expensive for his 3 paisas, and if they dropped or water fell on them, they would be ruined.

questions.question: What sweets did Hamid's friends buy after the toys?
options: Sesame seed candy, gulab-jamuns, or halva
questions.explanation: The text mentions that they bought sesame seed candy, gulab-jamuns, or halva and smacked their lips with relish.

questions.question: What did Hamid buy from the hardware store?
questions.explanation: Hamid bought a pair of tongs to help his grandmother in the kitchen.

questions.question: Why did Hamid choose to buy a pair of tongs?
options: He wanted to play with it as a musical instrument.
His granny burnt her fingers on the iron pan while cooking chappatis.
He wanted to break his friends' toys.
It was the only thing available for three paisas.
questions.explanation: He realized his granny didn't have tongs and burnt her fingers every time she cooked, so he bought it for her safety.

questions.question: What was the initial price quoted by the shopkeeper for the tongs?
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper first said it would cost six paisas, which made Hamid's heart sink.

questions.question: For how much did Hamid finally purchase the pair of tongs?
questions.explanation: Hamid boldly asked, 'Will you give it to me for three?' and the shopkeeper agreed.

questions.question: How did Hamid carry the tongs to show them off to his friends?
options: On his shoulder as if it were a gun
On his head like a cap
questions.explanation: Hamid carried the tongs on his shoulder as if it were a gun to impress his friends.

questions.question: What did Hamid compare his tongs to, saying it was superior to the clay toys?
questions.explanation: He said, 'My tongs are like a tiger among toys' because they were strong and unbreakable.

questions.question: Which of his friends offered to exchange a tambourine for the tongs?
questions.explanation: Sammi had bought a small tambourine worth eight paisas and wanted to exchange it, but Hamid refused.

questions.question: What was Granny Ameena's first reaction when she saw the tongs?
options: She scolded him for not eating and buying an iron tool.
questions.explanation: She initially called him a stupid child for starving till noon just to buy a pair of iron tongs.

questions.question: Why did Granny Ameena eventually start crying?
options: She was deeply moved by Hamid's selflessness and care for her.
She wanted him to buy sweets.
questions.explanation: When Hamid explained he bought it so she wouldn't burn her fingers, she realized his immense love and cried out of pure emotion.

questions.question: Complete the analogy based on the text: Mahmood : Policeman :: Mohsin : ________?
questions.explanation: Mahmood bought a Policeman, while Mohsin bought a Water-carrier.

questions.question: Add a suitable prefix to the word 'patient' to make its opposite.
questions.explanation: The correct prefix is 'im', making the word 'impatient'.

questions.question: Add a suitable suffix to the word 'wonder'.
questions.explanation: The correct suffix is 'ful', creating the word 'wonderful'.

questions.question: According to the vocabulary section, which word completes the sentence: 'The child was happy with his ________ of chocolates.'?
questions.explanation: A child usually keeps chocolates in his pocket, hence 'pocketful' is the appropriate quantity word here.

questions.question: Choose the correct determiner: 'There isn't ________ traffic on Sundays.'
questions.explanation: Traffic is an uncountable noun, so 'much' is the correct determiner to use in a negative context.

questions.question: Choose the correct determiner: 'Are there ________ mangoes in the basket?'
questions.explanation: In interrogative (question) sentences, we generally use 'any'.

questions.question: Identify the correct plural form of 'daughter-in-law' as noted in the grammar section.
questions.explanation: The 's' is added to the principal word 'daughter' to form the plural: daughters-in-law.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Hamid did not ride the merry-go-round.
Reason (R): Hamid was terrified of the wooden elephants and horses.
options: A is false, R is true
Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation
A is true, R is false
questions.explanation: Hamid didn't ride because he didn't want to waste his limited 3 paisas, not because he was terrified. So A is true, R is false.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Granny Ameena considered the tongs as precious as sacks of silver.
Reason (R): The tongs were actually made of pure silver.
options: A is true, R is false
A is false, R is true
Both A and R are true
Both A and R are false
questions.explanation: They were made of iron, but to Ameena, the love behind the gift made them 'as precious as sacks of silver'. So A is true, R is false.

questions.question: From the 'Do You Know?' section: How many members live in the Marapanhalli Laxmayha joint family?
questions.explanation: The text states there are 109 members in this massive joint family in Karnataka's Kolar district.

questions.question: In the Marapanhalli Laxmayha joint family, how many rooms does their single roof span across?
questions.explanation: 85 members live under a single roof that spans 20 rooms.

questions.question: Life-situation Question (HOTS): Imagine you have very little money at a fair, and you see your friends buying expensive toys. What does Hamid's behavior teach us in this situation?
options: It teaches us to feel jealous and angry.
It teaches us to borrow money from friends to fit in.
It teaches us self-control and the value of spending on something meaningful and useful.
It teaches us to buy cheap food instead of toys.
questions.explanation: Hamid resisted temptation and bought something highly useful for his grandmother, demonstrating immense maturity, self-control, and selflessness.

questions.question: HOTS Question: Why does the author describe Hamid’s tongs as a 'tiger among toys'?
options: Because it was painted with tiger stripes.
Because it was bought from a zoo.
Because it was durable, strong, and unbreakable unlike fragile clay toys.
Because it made a roaring sound.
questions.explanation: The tongs were made of iron. Hamid argued that if dropped, the clay toys would break, but his iron tongs would survive anything, making it superior and strong like a tiger.

questions.question: HOTS Question: Why did Granny Ameena's reaction change from anger to tears?
options: She realized the tongs were broken.
She understood that a four-year-old child sacrificed his own joy and hunger out of deep love and concern for her.
She felt bad for shouting loudly in front of neighbors.
She realized the tongs were very expensive.
questions.explanation: Her initial anger was out of concern for his hunger. Her tears fell when she realized the profound emotional weight of his selfless act.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'smack their lips with relish' indicate about Hamid's friends?
options: They were thoroughly enjoying the taste of the sweets they bought.
They did not like the sweets.
questions.explanation: To smack lips with relish means to eat something with great enjoyment and pleasure.

questions.question: Which of the following describes Hamid’s emotional state when the shopkeeper initially said 'six paisas' for the tongs?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Hamid's heart sank' because he had only three paisas and thought he couldn't afford it.

questions.question: What does the word 'wretched' refer to in the context: 'For the wretched woman, the pair of tongs was as precious...'
options: Unhappy and pitiful due to poverty
questions.explanation: Here, 'wretched' means unhappy or living in a miserable state of poverty, referring to Granny Ameena's hard life.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'gaped'?
options: To stare with an open mouth in wonder
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'gaped' as stared. The village children gaped at the town's wonders.

questions.question: Which figure of speech is used in the line: 'They descended on the sweet and toy-vendors’ stores like an army moving to attack'?
questions.explanation: The comparison uses the word 'like' to compare the rushing crowd to an attacking army, which is a Simile.
